payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields should capture enough information to reproduce cost, performance, and provider attribution for each evaluation run. Token counts should include prompt/input tokens, completion/output tokens, and total tokens; if available, also include cached, reasoning, audio, or tool-call tokens separately because pricing can differ by category. Provider and model IDs should record the exact provider name and model identifier used, including version or snapshot when available, so results are attributable and comparable over time. Estimated price should be computed from recorded token counts and the provider/model pricing table in effect at run time; it should include currency, unit rates, total estimated cost, and whether the value is estimated versus billed. Latency metrics should measure elapsed request time, usually in milliseconds, and report distribution percentiles across eval samples: p50 is the median latency, p90 is the latency below which 90% of requests completed, and p95 is the latency below which 95% of requests completed. These fields are required because they support regression tracking, cost control, provider/model comparisons, and debugging of slow or expensive runs.

entity_resolution.reason: The input asks for an explanation of evaluation accounting fields and does not provide a durable user memory to store.

details: Explained token accounting, provider/model identifiers, estimated price, and latency percentile fields.
